name: Around the World in Eighty Days

intro.title: Phileas Fogg's Eighty Days
intro.body:
Phileas Fogg wagered twenty thousand pounds at the Reform Club that he could circumnavigate the globe in eighty days, going EAST. Suez by the new canal, then Bombay, Calcutta, Hong Kong, Yokohama — and the Pacific crossing to San Francisco that no atlas of the day drew as a straight westward dash but as the eastward arc the steamship really took.

The directionality matters: a route engine that draws the shortest line between two points puts Yokohama-to-San-Francisco backward across Asia. Fogg's chronicle is the regression case — the route must cross the antimeridian.
